decision.name: Metric
decision.why: A metric displays an important number value prominently. You can add indicators or labels to help provide context to the presented value.

starter_code.label: Metric starter
notes: Canonical scaffold sources: /salt/patterns/metric

Supporting example sources: https://storybook.saltdesignsystem.com/?path=/story/patterns-metric--metric, https://www.figma.com/file/TI8zR16jnisL4W7ANedtUJ/(WIP)-Metric?type=design&node-id=652-3336&mode=design&t=UpxMBoYUQ8vMxz6z-0
Build around: Metric title; Metric value; Metric supporting context

Preserve constraints: Never leave the user guessing how to interpret your metric. Make sure you provide enough context, and use subtitles and indicators to minimize ambiguity.; Use the same orientation when displaying multiple metrics within a single view for greater visual consistency. Do not mix vertical and horizontal metrics.; Avoid displaying more than five metrics in a single dashboard view. This can clutter the user's screen and overwhelm them with too much information.
Optional scaffold regions: metric-supporting-context
Hybrid scaffold semantics were derived from docs/examples, but the fallback template is still hand-authored for implementation speed. Treat the linked docs as the source of truth.
Replace placeholder structure with the closest production example from the linked docs.
Starter code is based on the closest extracted pattern story example rather than a private fallback template.
Use the recommended JPM Brand theme bootstrap for new Salt work. Only fall back to legacy SaltProvider when migration compatibility or repo policy explicitly requires it.

slots.label: Metric Title
slots.reason: Metric Title is an optional Salt pattern region that becomes relevant when the task or supporting evidence calls for it. The current request and docs-backed scaffold point to Link, Stack layout here.
notes: Add Metric Title only when it improves the task flow or requested evidence.
Metric title
Metric value
Metric supporting context
Never leave the user guessing how to interpret your metric. Make sure you provide enough context, and use subtitles and indicators to minimize ambiguity.
Use the same orientation when displaying multiple metrics within a single view for greater visual consistency. Do not mix vertical and horizontal metrics.
Avoid displaying more than five metrics in a single dashboard view. This can clutter the user's screen and overwhelm them with too much information.

avoid: Never leave the user guessing how to interpret your metric. Make sure you provide enough context, and use subtitles and indicators to minimize ambiguity.
Use the same orientation when displaying multiple metrics within a single view for greater visual consistency. Do not mix vertical and horizontal metrics.
Avoid displaying more than five metrics in a single dashboard view. This can clutter the user's screen and overwhelm them with too much information.
